title: SonarApiClient: 429 rate-limit and HTML Content-Type PARSE_ERROR paths are untested
description: The `get()` private method in SonarApiClient handles HTTP 429 (→ ErrorType.RATE_LIMITED) and a non-JSON Content-Type (→ ErrorType.PARSE_ERROR). No test in SonarApiClientTest exercises either branch. A SonarQube instance behind a reverse proxy commonly returns 429 or an HTML error page (e.g. login redirect) on token expiry; without coverage these two error paths are dark.
suggestedFix:
Add two tests to SonarApiClientTest using MockWebServer:
1. `server.enqueue(MockResponse().setResponseCode(429))` → assert `result.isError && (result as ApiResult.Error).type == ErrorType.RATE_LIMITED`.
2. `server.enqueue(MockResponse().setBody("<html>Login</html>").addHeader("Content-Type", "text/html"))` → assert `result.isError && (result as ApiResult.Error).type == ErrorType.PARSE_ERROR`.
Both tests should call any endpoint, e.g. `client.validateConnection()`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. SonarApiClient.kt lines 549-551 handle HTTP 429 → ErrorType.RATE_LIMITED, and lines 526-533 detect non-JSON Content-Type → ErrorType.PARSE_ERROR. A full read of SonarApiClientTest.kt confirms no test exercises either branch. The existing test suite covers 401, 403 (componentExists), 404, and 503 (mid-pagination), but not 429 and not HTML content-type. The claimed severity of High is slightly overstated — these are real-world paths (rate limiting and HTML login-redirect pages from reverse proxies) but not crash-causing. Medium is more appropriate. Adding MockWebServer tests for both is safe and non-breaking.
recommendedAction: Add two tests to SonarApiClientTest: (1) enqueue a 429 response → assert ApiResult.Error with ErrorType.RATE_LIMITED; (2) enqueue a 200 with Content-Type: text/html → assert ApiResult.Error with ErrorType.PARSE_ERROR.

title: SonarApiClient: network IOException (offline) path is untested at the HTTP layer
description: The `get()` method catches `IOException` and maps it to `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR, ...)`. No test in SonarApiClientTest exercises this path — the service-layer tests stub with `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR, ...)` directly, bypassing the real HTTP stack. MockWebServer supports `SocketPolicy.DISCONNECT_AT_START` to simulate a TCP drop.
suggestedFix: Add a test: `server.enqueue(MockResponse().setSocketPolicy(SocketPolicy.DISCONNECT_AT_START))`, call `client.validateConnection()`, assert `result.isError && (result as ApiResult.Error).type == ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR`. Requires importing `mockwebserver3.SocketPolicy`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. SonarApiClient.kt lines 559-562 catch IOException and return ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR). SonarApiClientTest.kt has no test that exercises this path at the HTTP layer. The service-layer tests mock the ApiResult directly, bypassing the real client. MockWebServer's SocketPolicy.DISCONNECT_AT_START can simulate a TCP drop to trigger the IOException path. Claimed High severity is overstated — offline behavior is handled at a higher layer by FailureReason.OFFLINE — but the HTTP-layer path is still untested dark code. Medium is appropriate.
recommendedAction: Add a test: enqueue MockResponse with SocketPolicy.DISCONNECT_AT_START, call validateConnection(), assert result.isError and ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR.

title: SonarApiClient.getIssuesSinglePage: page/pageSize clamping and single-page behavior are untested
description: `getIssuesSinglePage()` was introduced as the F-15 fix for the agent's paged-issues path (one HTTP request with `&p=&ps=` instead of a full loop). It coerces page values with `coerceAtLeast(1)` and `coerceIn(1, ISSUES_PAGE_SIZE)`. There are zero tests for it: not the happy path, not the page/pageSize boundary clamping (e.g. page=0 → p=1, pageSize=501 → ps=500), and not its 401/404 error responses.
suggestedFix:
Add tests to SonarApiClientTest:
1. Happy path: enqueue a single-page response, call `getIssuesSinglePage("proj", 2, 10)`, assert the returned result contains the correct issues and that the request path includes `p=2&ps=10`.
2. Boundary clamping: call with `page=0, pageSize=999`, assert request path has `p=1&ps=500`.
3. Error response: enqueue 404, assert `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.NOT_FOUND, ...)`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. SonarApiClient.kt lines 210-228 implement getIssuesSinglePage with page.coerceAtLeast(1) and pageSize.coerceIn(1, ISSUES_PAGE_SIZE) clamping. A full read of SonarApiClientTest.kt confirms there are zero tests for this method — no happy path, no clamping boundary, no error response. The function is an agent-facing path (F-15 fix) and the clamping guards are safety-critical for avoiding invalid Sonar requests. Claimed High severity is reasonable for an agent-accessible path with zero test coverage. Downgrading slightly to Medium because the clamping is defensive-only and won't cause data loss.
recommendedAction: Add tests: (1) happy-path single page response, verifying p= and ps= in request URL; (2) boundary clamping (page=0, pageSize=999) → request has p=1&ps=500; (3) 404 response → ApiResult.Error with ErrorType.NOT_FOUND.

title: SonarApiClient.getMeasures: first-page error returns ApiResult.Error — that path is not tested
description: The measures pagination loop distinguishes 'first page error → propagate error' from 'mid-pagination error → return partial success'. The first-page-error path for `getMeasures` is not covered by any test. The equivalent path for `getIssuesWithPaging` (mid-pagination 503 → partial success) is tested, but first-page failure for `getMeasures` is not.
suggestedFix: Add a test: `server.enqueue(MockResponse().setResponseCode(401))`, call `client.getMeasures("proj")`, assert `result.isError && (result as ApiResult.Error).type == ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED`. Also add a mid-pagination 401 test: enqueue a valid page-1 response then a 401 for page-2 (with paging.total > 500 in page-1), assert `result.isSuccess` with partial data.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. getMeasures in SonarApiClient.kt (lines 325-347) has an explicit first-page-error path at line 341: `return if (page == 1) pageResult else ApiResult.Success(all)`. SonarApiClientTest.kt tests mid-pagination 503 for getIssuesWithPaging (line 417-431) and has multiple getMeasures pagination tests (lines 240-301), but none of them enqueue a 401/403/404 on page 1 for getMeasures. The first-page-error propagation is untested. The mid-pagination partial-success path for getMeasures is also untested. Both are real gaps. The severity is correctly Medium — not crash-level, but a missing contract test for a logic branch that explicitly distinguishes first vs. mid-pagination failure.
recommendedAction: Add tests: (1) enqueue 401, call getMeasures, assert ApiResult.Error with ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED (first-page failure propagates error); (2) enqueue a valid page-1 (with total > pageSize) then a 401 for page-2, assert ApiResult.Success with partial data (mid-pagination failure returns what was collected).

title: SonarDataService.getLineCoverage: no test for cache-hit, API error, blank projectKey, or concurrent fetch
description: The public `getLineCoverage` method has rich logic: it falls back to `settings.state.sonarProjectKey` when `projectKey` is null/blank, returns from `lineCoverageCache` on hit, calls `getSourceLines`, maps results via `CoverageMapper.mapLineStatuses`, and populates the cache. None of these paths have a test — not the cache-hit fast-path, not the `ApiResult.Error` returning `emptyMap()`, and not the blank/null projectKey fallback.
suggestedFix:
Add unit tests using a testable harness with a mocked SonarApiClient:
1. Cache-hit: pre-populate `lineCoverageCache`, call `getLineCoverage`, assert the result matches the cache value and no HTTP call was made.
2. API error: stub `getSourceLines` to return `ApiResult.Error(...)`, assert `getLineCoverage` returns `emptyMap()` and does NOT populate the cache.
3. Blank projectKey: pass `projectKey = ""` with no settings fallback, assert `emptyMap()` is returned immediately.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. SonarDataService.kt lines 232-259 implement getLineCoverage with three distinct early-exit paths: blank effectiveKey returns emptyMap() immediately (line 234), cache-hit returns cached value without HTTP (line 238), and ApiResult.Error returns emptyMap() without populating the cache (lines 255-258). A grep across all test files for getLineCoverage and lineCoverageCache returned zero results. The cache-hit path is particularly load-bearing: a bug there (e.g. accidentally returning null) would cause repeated API calls on every editor keystroke and break the CoverageLineMarkerProvider's fast path. Medium severity is accurate. Adding the tests is safe; no production change required.
recommendedAction: Add unit tests for getLineCoverage: (1) blank projectKey fast-exit, (2) cache-hit avoids HTTP call (pre-populate lineCoverageCache, verify no HTTP), (3) ApiResult.Error returns emptyMap and does not populate cache. Requires making the TestSonarDataService harness expose the lineCoverageCache or using a testable subclass.

title: SonarServiceImpl.getCoverage: coveredLines can silently go negative — no test for uncoveredLines > totalLinesToCover
description: In `SonarServiceImpl.getCoverage`, `coveredLines = totalLinesToCover - uncoveredLines` is computed without a floor at 0. If SonarQube returns inconsistent data (e.g. uncoveredLines reported from a different analysis epoch than linesToCover), `coveredLines` can be negative. The resulting `CoverageData(coveredLines = -N)` propagates to the agent's summary string. `SonarServiceImpl` has only partial test coverage via the `testClient` seam, and this particular method has no test at all.
evidence:
// SonarServiceImpl.kt lines 251-259
val totalLinesToCover = measures[SonarMetricKey.LINES_TO_COVER]?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
val uncoveredLines = measures[SonarMetricKey.UNCOVERED_LINES]?.toIntOrNull() ?: 0
val coveredLines = totalLinesToCover - uncoveredLines  // can be negative
val data = CoverageData(
    lineCoverage = lineCoverage,
    branchCoverage = branchCoverage,
    totalLines = totalLinesToCover,
    coveredLines = coveredLines  // negative passed through to agent
)
suggestedFix:
1. Fix: clamp `coveredLines = (totalLinesToCover - uncoveredLines).coerceAtLeast(0)`.
2. Add a test via the `testClient` seam (`SonarServiceImpl(...).also { it.testClient = api }`): stub `getProjectMeasures` with `lines_to_cover=10, uncovered_lines=15` (stale data scenario), call `getCoverage(...)`, assert `coveredLines == 0` (or whatever the clamped value should be).
3. Also add a basic happy-path test for `getCoverage`: this method currently has zero test coverage.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. SonarServiceImpl.getCoverage at line 253: coveredLines = totalLinesToCover - uncoveredLines with no coerceAtLeast(0) floor. When SonarQube returns inconsistent epoch data (uncoveredLines from a stale scan > totalLinesToCover from the current scan), coveredLines is negative. This propagates into the CoverageData.coveredLines field and appears verbatim in the agent's summary string at line 266: 'Covered: -N / M lines'. This is wrong output visible to users and agents, not a crash, so High is slightly inflated — Medium is correct. No test exists for getCoverage (confirmed by grep). The suggested coerceAtLeast(0) fix is non-harmful: SonarQube's own linesToCover/uncoveredLines data contract guarantees non-negative covered lines, so clamping at 0 is always semantically correct.
recommendedAction: Apply coveredLines = (totalLinesToCover - uncoveredLines).coerceAtLeast(0) at line 253. Add a getCoverage test via the testClient seam with lines_to_cover=10, uncovered_lines=15 (stale data) asserting coveredLines==0, and a happy-path test with normal data.

title: SonarDataService.refreshWith: CE-tasks FORBIDDEN branch (analysisHistoryForbidden=true) has no test
description: When `getAnalysisTasks` returns `ErrorType.FORBIDDEN`, `refreshWith` sets `analysisHistoryForbidden = true` in the resulting `SonarState`. No test verifies that this flag is correctly set, meaning the UI hint 'token lacks Administer Project permission' would go dark if the flag assignment were accidentally removed.
suggestedFix: In a TestSonarDataService harness (or extending the existing one in SonarDataServiceTest), stub `getAnalysisTasks` to return `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.FORBIDDEN, "403")` and assert `stateFlow.value.analysisHistoryForbidden == true`. Also verify that the state's `recentAnalyses` is empty (not the stale previous value).
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. The CE-tasks FORBIDDEN branch at SonarDataService.kt lines 446-448 sets analysisHistoryForbidden=true, but the TestSonarDataService in SonarDataServiceTest.kt (lines 143-206) is a hand-rolled test double whose simplified refreshWith omits the CE tasks fetch entirely. No test anywhere (searched all test files) exercises this path. The SonarDataServiceHotspotsTest.kt line 99 uses ErrorType.FORBIDDEN for the hotspots path, not the CE tasks path. Adding the suggested test would confirm the flag assignment. The fix is safe — adding a test does not alter production code. However, the UI hint going dark if the flag assignment were removed is a minor UX regression, not a data-loss or crash scenario; Medium is defensible but Low is more accurate given this is a display-hint path with no security or correctness impact.
recommendedAction: Add a test that stubs getAnalysisTasks to return ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.FORBIDDEN, ...) and asserts stateFlow.value.analysisHistoryForbidden == true. The TestSonarDataService harness must be extended to replicate the CE tasks logic branch from the real refreshWith.

title: SonarDataService.mapProjectHealth: no test for rating-letter mapping, partial metrics, or non-numeric values
description: The private `mapProjectHealth` method converts SonarQube numeric rating strings ("1.0".."5.0") via `SonarRatingUtils.ratingLetter`, and defaults all fields to 0/empty when the corresponding metric is missing. This method is tested only via the `refreshWith` integration path which uses an empty measures list (so all defaults). No test covers: a full metric set with real rating values, a non-numeric rating value, or missing individual metrics. `SonarRatingUtils.ratingLetter` itself also has zero dedicated tests.
suggestedFix: Add a dedicated `SonarRatingUtilsTest` covering: `ratingLetter("1.0") == "A"`, `ratingLetter("5.0") == "E"`, `ratingLetter("6.0") == ""` (out of range), `ratingLetter(null) == ""`, `ratingLetter("abc") == ""`. Also add a `mapProjectHealth` test within the DataService test double: supply all seven metrics with numeric ratings, assert each letter and numeric field is correctly populated.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. SonarRatingUtils.ratingLetter at lines 21-31 of SonarRatingUtils.kt converts numeric strings to A-E letters. The when block covers 1-5 correctly (2->B, 3->C, 4->D are present in the source, not just 1 and 5 as the finding claimed). Searching all test files for ratingLetter, SonarRatingUtils, and mapProjectHealth returns zero hits. The existing SonarDataServiceTest refreshWith tests always pass an empty measures list so mapProjectHealth always returns all-defaults. The risk of a bug here is contained — rating letters are display-only in the UI — which makes Low more appropriate than Medium. The fix is safe.
recommendedAction: Add a dedicated SonarRatingUtilsTest covering ratingLetter for all five valid inputs ("1.0"->"A" through "5.0"->"E"), out-of-range ("6.0"->""), null, and non-numeric ("abc"->""). Add a mapProjectHealth integration test within the TestSonarDataService that supplies all seven metrics and asserts each letter and numeric field.

title: SonarServiceImpl.compactLineNumbers: private helper has zero tests despite non-trivial logic
description: `compactLineNumbers` compresses a sorted line list into range strings (e.g. `[1,2,3,5,7,8]` → `"1-3, 5, 7-8"`). It has non-trivial branching: single-element runs, consecutive runs, non-consecutive gaps, and empty input. The method is private but embedded in the agent's LLM summary output, so a bug (e.g. off-by-one in `end + 1`) produces a silently wrong report. No test covers it directly or indirectly through `getBranchQualityReport`.
suggestedFix: Extract `compactLineNumbers` to an `internal` function (or a companion object) so it can be tested directly, or test it end-to-end through `getBranchQualityReport` by asserting specific substrings in the returned `summary` field. Required test cases: empty list → `""`, single element → `"42"`, consecutive range → `"1-3"`, single gap → `"1, 3"`, mixed → `"1-3, 5, 7-9"`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. compactLineNumbers at lines 1258-1275 of SonarServiceImpl.kt is private and contains non-trivial loop logic. SonarBranchReportGateAndDupTest.kt calls getBranchQualityReport but its test fixtures set new_uncovered_lines=0 (default setup line 57) and the tests only assert on isError, qualityGate.status, and duplicatedLineRanges — never on summary content containing line ranges. BranchQualityReportTest.kt only calls the mock API directly, not the real SonarServiceImpl. So compactLineNumbers is effectively untested end-to-end. However, the algorithm is straightforward (standard run-length encoding) and a regression here would produce a wrong-but-non-crashing report string. Low severity is appropriate.
recommendedAction: Extract compactLineNumbers to internal visibility (or a companion object) so it can be tested directly, or add an end-to-end test through getBranchQualityReport that supplies 3+ consecutive and non-consecutive uncovered line numbers and asserts specific substrings like '3-5' and '7' in the result summary.

title: SonarServiceImpl.componentExistsCached: TTL expiry and cache invalidation on URL change are untested
description: `componentExistsCached` in `SonarServiceImpl` caches `/api/components/show` results per `(sonarUrl, projectKey)` with a 5-minute TTL. The cache key is `ConnectionSettings.getInstance().state.sonarUrl to projectKey`, meaning a URL change in settings invalidates the key. No tests cover: (a) cache-hit short-circuit avoids a second HTTP call, (b) TTL expiry triggers re-fetch, or (c) stale URL in key means a URL change forces a fresh probe. The `SonarBranchReportGateAndDupTest` uses `testClient` but bypasses the `getIssues` → `componentExistsCached` code path because the test calls `getBranchQualityReport` (which does not call `componentExistsCached`).
suggestedFix:
Add tests to a new `SonarServiceImplIssuesTest` using the `testClient` seam:
1. Cache-hit: call `getIssues(...)` twice; stub `componentExists` to be called only once; assert the second call does not invoke `api.componentExists` (verify with `coVerify(exactly = 1)`).
2. Transient error fall-through: stub `componentExists` to return `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR, ...)`, assert `getIssues` proceeds to `api.getIssues` anyway (no error returned for a probe failure).
3. 'Not found' response: stub `componentExists` to return `ApiResult.Success(false)`, assert `getIssues` returns `ToolResult` with `isError = true` and a project-not-found message.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. componentExistsCached at lines 70-84 of SonarServiceImpl.kt maintains a ConcurrentHashMap with a 5-minute TTL, keyed by (sonarUrl, projectKey). The cache-hit early return at line 75 is reachable via getIssues but no test calls getIssues through the SonarServiceImpl at all — SonarBranchReportGateAndDupTest.kt only calls getBranchQualityReport (line 89/98/115), which does not go through componentExistsCached. The SonarApiClientTest tests the underlying componentExists HTTP call but not the caching layer. The risk is primarily correctness: a TTL bug would cause either stale 'not found' errors or unnecessary extra HTTP calls. Not a crash or data-loss scenario, so Low is appropriate.
recommendedAction: Add tests via the testClient seam (SonarServiceImpl.also { it.testClient = api }): call getIssues twice, stub componentExists to succeed on first call, use coVerify(exactly=1) to confirm the second call skips the HTTP probe. Also add tests for the not-found and transient-error fall-through paths.

title: SonarDataService.refreshWith: newCodeMode heuristic (branch-isMain detection) is untested
description: The `newCodeMode` resolution in `refreshWith` (lines 513-528) has three priority branches: (1) persisted setting (sonarPreferredCodeMode 1/2), (2) branch-isMain heuristic (new state when projectKey/branch changes: main → false, non-main → true), and (3) in-session toggle preserved. The TestSonarDataService double hard-codes `newCodeMode = false` in its state construction, so none of these branches are exercised.
suggestedFix: Extend the TestSonarDataService harness (or create a focused test class) that replicates the newCodeMode logic. Test cases: (a) `sonarPreferredCodeMode = 1` → `newCodeMode = false` regardless of branch type; (b) `sonarPreferredCodeMode = 2` → `newCodeMode = true`; (c) no setting, main branch → `newCodeMode = false`; (d) no setting, non-main branch → `newCodeMode = true`; (e) same projectKey/branch as previous state → in-session toggle value preserved.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. The newCodeMode resolution block at SonarDataService.kt lines 513-528 has three priority branches: persisted setting (sonarPreferredCodeMode 1 or 2), branch-isMain heuristic (new state only), and in-session toggle preserved. The TestSonarDataService in SonarDataServiceTest.kt (lines 143-206) completely omits this logic — its refreshWith hard-codes a fixed SonarState without newCodeMode computation. All five sub-cases (persisted=false, persisted=true, no-setting+main, no-setting+non-main, same key/branch toggle) are untested. However, this affects only the default tab selection in the UI Quality dashboard (Overall vs New Code view). Incorrect resolution would be a minor usability nuisance, not data corruption. Low severity is appropriate.
recommendedAction: Extend the TestSonarDataService harness to replicate the newCodeMode resolution logic, then add tests for all five branches: persisted=1 (false regardless of branch), persisted=2 (true regardless), no-setting+main branch (false), no-setting+non-main (true), same projectKey/branch (in-session toggle preserved).

title: SonarApiClient.paginateSonarItems: MAX_ISSUES_PAGES hard-cap path is not tested
description: The `paginateSonarItems` helper includes a post-loop return at line 296 that fires when MAX_ISSUES_PAGES (20) is exhausted before `allItems.size >= total`. This 'truncated result' path has a log.warn but returns `ApiResult.Success` with partial data. No test exercises it for either issues or hotspots — the existing pagination tests all stop before the cap.
suggestedFix: Add a test that sets up `MAX_ISSUES_PAGES + 1` pages worth of data but declares a `total` larger than `MAX_ISSUES_PAGES * ISSUES_PAGE_SIZE`. Because actually enqueueing 21 responses is impractical, override `MAX_ISSUES_PAGES` or use a tiny custom wrapper. Alternatively, test the cap at a reduced page count by creating a `SonarApiClient` with a known-small page constant. Assert: (a) exactly `MAX_ISSUES_PAGES` requests are made, (b) result is `ApiResult.Success` with `MAX_ISSUES_PAGES * pageSize` items.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed. paginateSonarItems at lines 264-298 of SonarApiClient.kt has three exit paths: (a) allItems.size >= total early-stop (tested by getIssuesWithPaging stops test at line 401), (b) mid-pagination error partial-success (tested at line 417), and (c) MAX_ISSUES_PAGES=20 cap exhaustion at lines 296-297 (not tested). The finding is accurate — no test enqueues 20+ pages with a total > 20*pageSize. The cap path returns ApiResult.Success with partial data and a log.warn, which is the correct graceful-degradation behavior. The risk is that a future refactor could accidentally change the Success return to an Error, which would break large projects silently. However this is a real-world edge case (>10,000 issues per project) and the behavior is not user-visible beyond truncated results. Low severity is appropriate; the finding correctly notes the impracticality of enqueuing 21 responses and suggests overriding MAX_ISSUES_PAGES.
recommendedAction: Add a test that constructs SonarApiClient with a reduced MAX_ISSUES_PAGES constant (or tests paginateSonarItems indirectly via a small reflectively-set cap), enqueues cap+1 pages worth of items declared at a total larger than cap*pageSize, and asserts the result is ApiResult.Success with exactly cap*pageSize items and that exactly cap requests were made.

title: CoverageMapper.mapLineStatuses: coveredConditions=null with non-null conditions edge case not tested
description: In `mapLineStatuses`, the PARTIAL branch triggers when `coveredConditions != null && coveredConditions < conditions`. However, if `conditions` is non-null but `coveredConditions` IS null (a valid API response when the branch count is present but no coverage info is available), the line falls through to the `else -> LineCoverageStatus.COVERED` branch — which is arguably wrong (it should be PARTIAL or UNCOVERED for a line with uncovered branches). There is no test for this combination.
suggestedFix:
Add a test to CoverageMapperTest:
```kotlin
val lines = listOf(SonarSourceLineDto(line = 1, lineHits = 1, conditions = 2, coveredConditions = null))
val result = CoverageMapper.mapLineStatuses(lines)
// Document the current behavior — either assert COVERED (current) to pin it,
// or fix to PARTIAL and assert PARTIAL.
```
The fix should likely treat `conditions != null && coveredConditions == null` as PARTIAL since branch data is present but untested.
verdictReasoning: The code path at CoverageMapper.kt lines 46-51 is confirmed: the `when` block short-circuits on `coveredConditions != null` before comparing to `conditions`, so a DTO with `conditions=2, coveredConditions=null, lineHits>0` falls through to `else -> LineCoverageStatus.COVERED`. Both fields are nullable in SonarSourceLineDto (lines 507-508 of SonarDtos.kt), so the combination is structurally possible. The test file confirms no test exercises this combination. However, the SonarQube `/api/sources/lines` API consistently returns `coveredConditions=0` (not omitted) when a line has branch conditions but none are covered — this `null`+non-null pairing does not arise from a real SonarQube response in normal operation. The finding is real as an untested edge-case code path, but the described misclassification (COVERED instead of PARTIAL) has no practical impact because the triggering API shape is not emitted by SonarQube. The suggested fix — adding a test to document the current behavior — is purely additive and not harmful. The claimed severity of Low is accurate.
recommendedAction: Add a test asserting the current behavior (COVERED) to pin the edge-case contract, accompanied by a comment explaining that SonarQube does not emit conditions without coveredConditions. Do not change the production logic unless a real SonarQube response that triggers this path is demonstrated.
